warp-form.system_text:
If you succeed, [[ warp-form-happy-accidents | the target receives [[warp-form-86 | a new [[rewrite-dna| Battle Scar]] ]] of your choosing,]] limited by the contested Outcome:
  1. A Minor Battle Scar E.g. an aesthetic-only body modification (a disfigured face, a pig's tail), impacted hand or foot, or vocal changes that complicate communication
  2. A Major Battle Scar E.g. a disabled/removed hand or foot, an inability to speak, an impacted major sense, removal of a minor sense, impacted movement.
    3. [[warp-form-pesky |
  3. A Severe Battle Scar E.g. a disabled/removed arm or leg, deafness, aesthetic changes that are nearly impossible to conceal.
    4. ]] [[warp-form-pesky |
  4. An Extreme Battle Scar E.g. removal/disabling both hands or both legs, blindness, a full-body deformation that leaves the target humanoid but makes it difficult to use clothes, vehicles, and equipment designed for humans.
    5. ]] [[warp-form-lethal]]
Refer to the stock Battle Scars on the Character Sheet for specific systems. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

terrain-control.name: Terrain Control

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
Location
create ((terrain-control-type|modifications to terrain))
spend an Action
Attempting to break any alterations with inappropriate tools may damage or destroy the tools at the GM's discretion.
Any structures you create leave precise gaps for obstacles in their path or push them out of the way. You cannot directly destroy objects or structures or damage beings by creating walls or domes.

Tool guidance for GMs:
  • [[commune-animals]]
  • Difficulty 4-5: Power tools, vehicles, explosives.

Difficulty 9: Brawn 5, no tools.
  • Difficulty 7-8: One-handed tools or makeshift tools (like a sword). Mythic Brawn without tools.
  • Difficulty 6: Appropriate manual tools. Crowbar, sledgehammer, armor-shredding attacks.
This Effect produces the substance you are controlling unless it is drawn from the environment via “Required Environment” Drawbacks.

terrain-control.system_text:
Select one of the following alterations to create out of [[terrain-material]] originating at the target: ;;terrain-control-type-description|Select at least one "Control Type" Enhancement//

[[terrain-fast-form | The alteration begins to form when you activate this Effect and finishes forming on your initiative in the next Round. Until it finishes forming, it is not guaranteed to function.]]

This alteration lasts [[duration]] but may be prematurely destroyed or altered. Your alterations may be broken through with appropriate tools. Anyone attempting to break through must roll Brawn + a relevant Ability. A cumulative Outcome of !!potency,terrain-fragile-multiplier** across all attempts breaks a hole large enough to move through. See the extended system text for GM guidance on various tools. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

telekinesis.name: Telekinesis
telekinesis.description: You are able to move objects at a distance without making physical contact. You must maintain full concentration and line of sight or the effect will end.

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
spend an Action
interact with the world without physically touching it
Lasts two hours.
the ability to interact with the world without physically touching it
Weapons: [[duel-wits | You cannot wield weapons effectively.]]
Levitation: If you are strong enough to lift yourself, you may levitate up to [[activation-range]] away from the ground. Movement is limited to 5 feet per Round.

Lifting and hauling: You may lift !!mind-strength,lifting-multiplier** pounds. You may move targets you lift up to [[activation-range]] with an Action or half that distance as a Free Action. [[size-matters-not]] Animate targets may Resist being moved unless you have successfully Grappled them.
Combat: [[forceful-mind | You cannot deal Damage through unarmed Attacks or thrown objects using this Effect. You may still Grapple or Defend.]]
Fine Tools: [[fine-tools | You are limited to Actions a normal human could perform with their bare hands.]]
Any telekinetic Actions which interact with Animate targets may require contested rolls at GMs discretion.
Line of Sight: [[out-of-sight | You must have line of sight on anything you interact with. If a target you are Concentrating on moves completely out of sight, your Concentration is disrupted.]]
Multiple Targets: [[mental-juggling | You can only interact with one thing or take one Action at a time.]]

suggestion.name: Suggestion
suggestion.summary: Order someone to do what you want
suggestion.description: You have the capacity to issue a command to someone that they are forced to follow. The command must be verbalized or otherwise directly communicated and cannot force someone to endanger themselves or others, or significantly compromise their core beliefs.

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
compel a target to follow your orders
spend at least one Action

an order to take an immediate Action
The target must be able to understand you and must receive your communication for the suggestion to work.
Allowed Commands ;;suggestion-command-errata//
Direct Order: The target must take an immediate action (e.g. “Sit down,” "run away," or "tell me your password.")
Select a command that for your trap to issue.
Roll [[consumable-roll-dice|7 dice]] [[activation-difficulty | Difficulty 6]], dice penalties do not apply.
[[self-destructive | Your target cannot be engaged in Combat.]]
In the case of a failed activation, GMs should consider how bystanders would realistically react to a potentially-outlandish accusation of mind-reading.

suggestion.system_text:
[[succeed-grammar-override | If the contested Outcome is positive]], [[aoe-targets | your [[multi-target | target]]]] will be compelled to follow your command [[suggest-letter-law | to their best understanding of the letter and spirit of the command]] until they have completed it or for your [[duration]]. You cannot issue another suggestion to the target until 10 minutes after they have completed the first command or 10 minutes after a failure.

[[unreliable-agent | The target is unaware that they are under supernatural influence and feel they are acting of their own free will.]] If you fail, they realize that you were attempting to influence them supernaturally with your command.

Your command can be ((suggestion-direct,suggestion-conditional,suggestion-goal)). You cannot order a target to "do nothing," and they are free to take other Actions as they fulfill your request. Your command cannot be obviously self-destructive. [[suggest-limit | The Effect ends if following your command would cause a target to cross one of their Limits in a way that would necessitate a Trauma roll.]] {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

stash.name: Stash
stash.summary: Store items in a hidden location
stash.description: You are able to store things in a way such that they are always accessible to you but no one else. Items are generally not visible while stashed away.

substitutions.replacement: The Maximum Object Size parameter is intended to roughly capture the weight / size restriction of items and isn’t intended to restrict shape. GM discretion is advised.  A human being is approximately 60 liters.
spend an Action

Containers which hold multiple things may count as a single item so long as the things within are generally packaged together. For instance, you may stash a clip full of bullets, a medical kit full of medical supplies, or a box of crackers, but you cannot stash a backpack full of miscellaneous equipment.
store things in a hidden stash
You may only attempt to store Objects which are free-standing and disconnected from other things. The use of this Effect will not allow you to sever any connections holding an Object in place.

possession.name: Possession
possession.description:
You are able to temporarily transfer your consciousness into someone, possessing them.  They remain aware but helpless during the ordeal. 

Forcing them to perform actions that violate their most deeply-held convictions makes it easier for them to force you out. 

If the body you are possessing or the body you left behind dies, you die.

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
spend an Action
Living
Sapient
Access to equipment-based Gifts such as Signature Items depends entirely on whether you are holding the relevant object, and is not impacted by whether or not you are possessing a target.
possess a target's body
Roll [[consumable-roll-dice|7 dice]] [[activation-difficulty | Difficulty 6]], penalty does not apply.

possession.system_text:
If you succeed, you possess your target for the [[duration]]. [[black-out | The target has no control over their body or actions, though they retain consciousness and will remember anything that occurs during the possession.]] [[weird-walk]]

[[no-body | Your original body will be left behind, unconscious.]] [[death-needs-a-body | You may perceive through your original body's senses by Concentrating. If your original body is touched, you may roll Perception + Alertness to notice.]] You may spend an Action to end the possession early.

Stats: During the possession, you use the host body’s Body rating, but your own Mind rating. Any rolls you make use the host body’s Brawn, Dexterity, and Perception but your own [[muscle-memory | Charisma, Intellect, and Abilities]]. You gain the effects of any physical Assets and Liabilities they have. [[open-book | You cannot read the target’s mind or memories.]]

Gifts: [[self-powers | You have access to and can use any of your own Powers which are [[empty-no-body | not]] intrinsic to your biology or physical body.]] [[possessed-powers | You cannot access any Powers or other supernatural effects which the target has, unless they are intrinsic to the target’s biology or physical body.]]

Death: [[sympathetic-pain]] If the host body is killed or destroyed while you are still possessing it, [[emergency-recall | you will die along with it.]] [[possessive-spirit | If the possession ends and your original body was killed or destroyed, you die.]]

Eviction: [[instant-eviction]] [[possession-tether]] [[contest-will]] If you attempt to force your host to take actions which would violate one of their Limits or their instinct for self-preservation, they may resist by rolling Mind. If their Outcome is higher than or equal to the contested Outcome, the possession ends and you are evicted from their body. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

[[visual-concealed-override | [[visual-power-alien]]  When activating this Effect, it is obvious you are interacting with the target.]] [[weird-walk]]
[[visual-item-use-requirement]]

obscure.name: Obscure
obscure.description:
You have the ability to temporarily conceal yourself from a single sense, chosen at Power creation. While active, you are completely concealed from the chosen sense, unless it is vision. In that case, you are camouflaged, not invisible, so a keen eye may still spot you. 

This Power is not obviously supernatural.
obscure.eratta:
Cannot be taken with powers that make you difficult to hear, suppress other senses, etc.

If you use the Switch enhancement, and enter combat or are injured while the power is temporarily deactivated, it will end the effect entirely and you will need to reapply it (unless you have Combat Stealth).

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
spend an Action

GMs should use their discretion when determining the exact impacts of this Effect, particularly in conjunction with environmental factors. Standing still against a complex background may render you impossible to detect, and footprints in snow may make it easy. A chance to detect you is not guaranteed.

[[specific-illusion | The chosen illusion]] is generated at [[self-centered | your target]], and it will remain in place [[duration]] or you choose to end it. It can be perceived by people through [[illusion-all-senses | their ((illusion-senses))]]. The illusion cannot be used as an attack. [[illusive-man | You cannot make an exact replica of an existing Animate being or Object.]]

Anyone who [[illusion-break-condition | touches the illusion]] [[illusion-break-other-senses | (or who would be able to perceive it significantly through a sense it cannot fool)]] [[dispel-illusion | realizes it is an illusion and can choose to perceive it or not at will. ]]

[[traveling-illusion | The illusion will remain fixed to its initial target, and can only change locations if the initial target is mobile in some way.]] It may have moving parts and engage in simple, predetermined movements but cannot perform anything complex or interactive unless you maintain Concentration and “puppeteer” it. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

hack.description: You may issue an arbitrary command to any piece of technology you are interfacing with. This command can be a request for a single set of credentials, allowing you to hack a specific account until its passcode is changed.

substitutions.replacement: issue commands to technological devices
Exert your Mind
Computer

Any commands you issue must be a single “action” (e.g. initiating a bank transfer, retrieving certain data, deactivating an account, shutting down). Your command must be within the target’s current capabilities. For example, you cannot order a standard security camera to grow legs and walk around.
The credentials obtained/bypassed with this Effect are a complete set and are not thwarted by 2-factor authentication, etc.
[[network-hack-errata|You cannot hack things outside of your range over remote connections such as the internet. The system you are affecting must be physically within range.]]
Non-Alien
If the target has mundane security, no roll is required. If it has Alien security, you must
Roll [[consumable-roll-dice|7 dice]], contested by the target’s security dice. [[activation-roll-difficulty]] Stress penalties do not apply.
Your Difficulty depends on your desired level of access (4-5 for routine commands or single user credentials, 6-7 for system-wide denial or access). [[remote-hack-difficulty]]

This Power grants access to an array of effects relating to the usage of standard firearms. It can also be used with the Unique Weapon Drawback to acquire a new gun. In that case, the base stats for that firearm should be based off of an existing mundane firearm, and it should not behave much differently. 

This power is not obviously supernatural.
firearms-mastery.eratta: As a general rule, no bonuses stack. For example, if you have armor penetration from your firearms master and your enchanted bullets, only the larger bonus applies. The same goes for dice bonuses and difficulty modifiers.

[[trap-fascinate-end| If [[self-centered | you]] pass from their perception and they are unable to pursue you, the Effect ends. If they can follow you, they must do so for 2 Rounds or the Effect will end.]] [[combat-taunt | The Effect ends early if the target is Injured or violence occurs nearby.]]

emotion-control.name: Emotion Control
emotion-control.description: You can temporarily inspire an emotional shift in a target, often leading to some action they otherwise wouldn't have taken. This Power works best on the weak-minded.
emotion-control.eratta: The exact way in which the emotional shift plays out is up to the GM (or to the other player if cast on a player character), and as such there is a lot of variation in outcome for this power.  GM's, integrity is crucial when a player uses this power.  Your NPCs do not get an inherent resistance to this power just because they're stoic.  They don't need to outwardly show that fear has been induced in them, but their behavior will certainly change based on the sense of fear they now have.

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
spend an Action
as well as a specific emotion
Affected targets can resist by rolling Mind at [[defensive-difficulty | Difficulty 6]].
Living
inflame the emotions of others

Common emotions and the actions they inspire
  • Anger (shouting, cruelty, denying requests, attacking)
  • Pity (stopping an attack, executing an injured target)
  • Happiness (generosity, dancing, joking, socializing)
  • Sadness (finding solitude, openly crying, demotivation)
  • Fear (running, attacking)
Roll [[consumable-roll-dice|7 dice]] [[activation-difficulty | Difficulty 6]], dice penalties do not apply.
If you succeed, the target MUST take at least one Action they otherwise would not have. Hey, GM who is reading this, I'm talking to you. You have to enforce this. Remember: Gifts must feel powerful.

The disguise lasts for two hours, or you may end it early at will.
"Appearance" includes voice, bone structure, skin quality, hair length and color, eye color, muscle tone, flesh texture, and generally anything perceivable about your body.

If a disguise is used to conceal a persistent visibility requirement for another Effect, it ends as soon as you gain any benefit from the Effect. Disguise cannot be used to conceal visibility requirements for effect activations or similar.

disguise.system_text:
[[jekyll-hyde | You become disguised in a manner of your choosing.]] [[duration]] [[cheap-costume]]

[[alter-ego | The new appearance]] [[shape-shift | must have a similar sex, age, and race to your own. You cannot significantly change your height and weight.]] [[mimic-ditto | Your disguise cannot directly mimic an existing person.]] [[change-clothes | You cannot alter your clothes.]] [[slightly-monstrous]] [[deep-fake]] A disguise cannot affect your Attributes or other stats.

If you are not already Beautiful, you may roll Charisma + Crafts to attempt to make your disguise Beautiful. If your Outcome is 6 or higher, non-Effect Charisma rolls where your good looks could help are rolled at -2 Difficulty. If you fail or botch, you are ugly instead, and non-Effect Charisma rolls are made at +2 Difficulty. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}
disguise.system_errata: The changes caused by this Effect may be real flesh and blood or a flawless illusion, depending on your desired flavor.

[[visual-item-use-requirement]]

While transformed, you cannot use any equipment [[ ctf-artifact-use | (including Artifacts and Consumables)]], [[ctf-powers | and you do not have access to any of your Powers.]] [[ctf-au-natural | Any equipment you are wearing transforms with you.]]

Injuries and Wound Level are carried over between forms. However, transforming can never kill you; you merely remain Incapacitated until your Injuries are sufficiently healed. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

spend an Action or Reaction

animal-possession.system_text:
You possess your target [[possession-passenger | and have control over their actions]] [[duration]]. [[look-dont-touch]] [[no-body | Your original body will be left behind, unconscious.]] [[death-needs-a-body | You may perceive through your original body's senses by maintaining Concentration. If your original body is touched, you may roll Perception + Alertness to notice.]] [[possession-tether]] You may spend an Action to end the possession early. [[new-host]]

Stats: Any Actions you take will use the possessed creature’s Brawn, Dexterity, and Perception, but use your own Charisma, Intellect, and Abilities.

Gifts: [[mage-creature | You do not have access to any Gifts while you are possessing your target.]]

Death: [[possession-eviction | If the host suffers an Injury, or is destroyed or incapacitated, you are evicted back into your own body.]] [[death-needs-a-body | If the possession ends and your original body was killed or destroyed, you will die.]]

Eviction: [[possession-self-harm | You cannot force your host to take Actions which would violate their instinct for self-preservation (which includes engaging in Combat for any purpose other than self-defense or natural hunting); any attempt to do so will sever the connection and send you back into your own body.]] [[smooth-exit | If you are forced out of the target's body prematurely, you must make a Trauma roll.]]

Targets with Body ratings higher than 10 require a Charisma + Animals roll resisted by the target’s Body. Kaiju and environment-scale targets are immune. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

When scanned or investigated without opening, the contents of this container appear normal and unremarkable. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

binding-oath.name: Binding Oath
binding-oath.summary: Craft deals that punish those who break them

substitutions.replacement: All Oaths must have at least two participants.
A Battle Scar up to Severe (deaf, missing limb)
The oath-breaker is physically marked as an oath-breaker, and any investigative effect will reveal the terms of the oath they broke.
A particular Trauma and a single Mind Damage
The oath-breaker goes unconscious for up to 24 hours
[[range | The target must be physically present to enter the oath.]]
craft binding oaths
An Injury up to Severity-6 (cannot be reduced by any means)
The terms and penalties of the oath do not need to be equitable and do not need to affect all parties equally.
A single action cannot break multiple oaths. You cannot 'stack' oath penalties by having a target sign multiple identical oaths, or similar.
You cannot enter an oath with yourself.

binding-oath.system_text:
You may make an oath with [[oath-target-multiple| a]] [[range | physically-present,]] Sapient [[target-noun | target]]. Communicate the oath's terms to the [[target-noun | target]], including the requirements and penalties for each participant. If all agree to the terms, you must [[primary-activation-cost| Exert your Mind]] and all participants must [[way-to-seal-deal]] to seal the deal.

Record the oath as a Condition. If a party breaks the oath, the Condition ends and they suffer the oath's penalty.

When crafting your oath, you may incorporate any of the following penalties: ;;oath-penalty//

The [[target-noun | target]] cannot be compelled to agree via a direct threat of violence, another Effect, or another oath. If you would like to use clever wording to mislead the [[target-noun | target]], you must succeed a contested Intellect + (Culture or Influence) roll. {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}
binding-oath.system_visibility: [[visual-concealed-override | [[visual-power-subtle]] The target must understand that you are presenting the terms of an agreement and that they are agreeing to those terms. Witnesses understand the same.]] [[visual-item-use-requirement]]

within arm's reach

Living

Example environments for adaptation: space, all saltwater, all fresh water, all water under 50 feet deep, all water deeper than 50 feet, the jungle, radioactive areas, a specific alien world, etc.

augment-other.system_text:
You may add one augmentation to your target. Each augmentation counts as a [[brain-hacks|Battle Scar]] and can have exactly one of the following Effects:
  • Specialized: You receive [[bnw-specialized|+2 dice]] to a particular non-combat, non-Effect Action.
  • Environmental Adaptation: You do not suffer harm from heat, cold, pressure, or lack of oxygen in a particular environment, provided that challenge normally exists in that environment.
  • Implanted Tool: You have access to the functionality of some common Device at all times. The selected Device can be no larger than [[bnw-implanted-tool-size|a motorcycle helmet]].
  • Armor: You have [[bnw-armor|2]] Armor at all times. As always, Armor from multiple sources does not stack.
  • Pouch: Part of your body functions as a hidden pocket. It can hold no more than [[bnw-implanted-tool-size|a briefcase (15 liters)]].
  • Zippy: Your Free Movement is increased by [[bnw-zippy|10 feet]].
  • Maws and Claws: Your unarmed attacks do [[bnw-maws|+2]] Weapon Damage instead of -1.
[[after-care-regimen]] {{additional-effects}} {{additional-restrictions}}

animate.name: Animate
animate.description: You can animate inanimate objects, allowing them to move and perform a simple task for a short duration.
animate.eratta:
The mobility bestowed by Animate can include floating and flying for the affected objects, but should never provide mobility to characters. For example, an animated broom may float up to the rafters to sweep, but the caster cannot ride upon it. The sole exception to this rule are inanimate objects that are designed for transport, such as a car (which could drive with passengers, on the ground) or planes (which may fly with passengers, as they normally would).

This ability cannot be used to bypass security measures in place on the object (unlock phones or open locks or handcuffs).

substitutions.replacement: Exert your Mind
spend an Action
Awareness: Animated targets are capable of perceiving the world around them within reasonable limits. [[animate-communication | They cannot communicate in a clear or coherent way.]]

Movement: Animated targets can use an existing method of locomotion (wheels, etc); otherwise they can [[movement-speed | move across the ground at 15 feet per round, or hover [[hover-height | up to a height of 7 feet in the air]] at 10 feet per round.]]
Inanimate
bring inanimate objects to life
A task for which an object was designed might include a broom sweeping, a gun shooting things, a car driving, a towel rubbing up on stuff, etc.

Animated objects may be more or less susceptible to certain attacks at GMs discretion. A scarecrow golem would be easily destroyed by a sword (or fire). An animated suit of armor may not fear bullets but could be smashed to bits with a hammer. A full bronze statue is largely indestructible but might have difficulty standing up if toppled.
Artifacts: [[animate-artifacts | Animating an Artifact does not grant the animated object access to any Effects built in to that Artifact.]]
Combat: [[combat-skills | Combat: Animated objects cannot Defend on behalf of others or take offensive actions in Combat.]]

Ability Use: [[skill-level | If an action requires a roll, Animated objects have a dice pool of 7 when performing a task for which they were designed, and a dice pool of 4 for taking any other actions.]] [[additional-skill-text]]
Toughness: Animated targets are as easy to destroy as they were prior to being animated. If destroyed, the effect ends.
Following Orders: [[animate-complexity | Animated objects are controlled by the GM, and will follow any commands you give them, as long as they don't require problem-solving.]]
If an indestructible object is animated, the animation ends in any event where the base object would have been destroyed.

[[visual-item-use-requirement]]

channeled-fireball.name: Channeled Fireball
channeled-fireball.description: You may maintain Concentration after activating this Effect. If there remains a clear path between you no longer than your range, even a curved one, for one entire Round, you deal the same amount of Damage again, plus your base Damage.
channeled-fireball.eratta: The path between you and your target is obvious while Concentrating. You can Concentrate as long as you like to continue dealing Damage, but the base Damage stacks only once. You pay the activation costs of this Effect once. The original rolls stand, and the target cannot React to the additional rounds of Damage.

substitutions.replacement: Channeled Fireball: The path between you and your target is obvious while Concentrating. You can Concentrate as long as you like to continue dealing Damage, but the base Damage stacks only once. You pay the activation costs of this Effect once. The original rolls stand, and the target cannot React to the additional rounds of Damage.

You may maintain Concentration after activating this Effect. If there remains a clear path between you no longer than [[range]] for one entire Round, you deal the same amount of Damage again, plus ##damage,extra-damage++. The path may have one curve in it.

force-field.name: Force Field
force-field.description: You may Exert your Mind and spend an Action or Reaction to ignore all Damage and destructive Effects against your vehicle this Round.

substitutions.replacement: Force Field: You may Exert your Mind and spend an Action or Reaction to ignore all Damage and destructive Effects against your vehicle this Round.
You also gain the following effects:

familiar.name: Familiar
familiar.description: Your Companion has access to one of your Activated or Targeted Powers. If you choose a Passive Power, only one of you can benefit at a time, and you can switch who has access to it as a Free Action.
familiar.eratta: You and your companion cannot activate the same Effect on the same Round. You cannot choose a Spawn Minion Effect.

substitutions.replacement: Your companion has access to $. Only one of you can benefit from a given Passive Power at a time, and you can switch who has access as a Free Action. You and your companion cannot activate the same Effect on the same Round.

reach.name: Reach
reach.description: Your appendages have 30 feet of reach.
reach.eratta: You may stretch, retract, or move your limbs a great distance once per Round.

substitutions.replacement: You may stretch, retract, or move your limbs 30 feet once per Round.
Your appendages have 30 feet of reach.

the-hunger.name: The Hunger
the-hunger.description: The raised creature lasts forever but is required to consume a specific thing every day, or they will return back to death.
the-hunger.eratta: Requires GM approval. The specific thing they are required to consume should be at least moderately difficult for the creature to obtain, or impose some level of restriction on it's behavior - for example, eating human flesh requires an actual source of human flesh, and comes with clear logistical challenges, whereas eating dirt does not.

substitutions.replacement: The raised creature must consume $ every day or it will die again.
The creature will die at the end of any 24 hour period gone without consuming their specified diet, and it is important that this growing desperation is reflected in how they are roleplayed.

prodigy.name: Prodigy
prodigy.description: Whenever you witness someone get an Outcome of 6 or higher, or someone demonstrates an Ability with the intent of teaching you, you may Exert your Mind. If you do, rolls using that Ability use their rating instead of your own for the next day.

substitutions.replacement: Prodigy: Whenever you witness someone get an Outcome of 6 or higher, or someone demonstrates an Ability with the intent of teaching you, you may Exert your Mind. If you do, rolls using that Ability use their rating instead of your own for the next day.
You also gain the following effects:

possess-object.name: Possess Object
possess-object.description: Instead of transforming into an Object, you possess a touched Object. You leave no body behind.

substitutions.replacement: disappear into a touched object, possessing it
possess objects

reach-extra-appendage.name: Reach
reach-extra-appendage.description: Your extra appendage can stretch to reach an additional 30 feet.
reach-extra-appendage.eratta: You may stretch, retract, or move your limbs a great distance once per Round.

substitutions.replacement: Your extra appendage can stretch to reach an additional 30 feet.
You may stretch, retract, or move your limbs 30 feet once per Round.

extend-o-blade.name: Extend-o Blade
extend-o-blade.description: This melee weapon may extend up to 50 feet for a single Attack, Defense, or Clash. Retracting back into its usable size is a Quick Action.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:
Extend-o-blade: This melee weapon may extend up to 50 feet for a single Attack, Defense, or Clash. Retracting back into its usable size is a Quick Action.

seeing-double.name: Seeing Double
seeing-double.description: The effective range of all your senses are multiplied by your rating in Extra Perception + 1. This does not allow you to ignore environmental factors and excess noise.
seeing-double.eratta: The additional sensory input should never decrease the effectiveness of a sense. For senses without explicit ranges, GMs should consider how far a normal person could sense the input given current conditions and then multiply by that amount. For example, if you have to be within 5 feet to hear someone shouting your name in a noisy nightclub, multiply that distance.

substitutions.replacement: The additional sensory input should never decrease the effectiveness of a sense. For senses without explicit ranges, GMs should consider how far a normal person could sense the input given current conditions and then multiply by that amount. [[seeing-double-example]]
For example, if you have to be within 5 feet to hear someone shouting your name in a noisy nightclub, multiply that distance.
You also gain the following effects:
Seeing Double: The ranges of all your senses are multiplied by ##extra-perception,plus-one++. This does not allow you to ignore environmental factors and excess noise.

fashionista.name: Fashionista
fashionista.description: You may alter your outfit's appearance.
fashionista.eratta: This does not grant or store Equipment. This does not allow you to change the appearance of Artifacts or conceal their Alien appearance.

substitutions.replacement: You may change the appearance of your outfit.
While you may alter your outfit's appearance, this does not grant or store equipment. This does not allow you to change the appearance of Artifacts or conceal their Alien appearance.

substitutions.replacement: You may activate a single Effect through your ward as though you were standing at its location. Doing so destroys the ward.

bubble-barrier.name: Bubble Barrier
bubble-barrier.description: When you activate this Effect, you may opt to erect a bubble barrier. If you do so, no physical objects or entities can pass into your bubble until the barrier is broken, but you and the barrier cannot move.
bubble-barrier.eratta: Extreme impacts count as Damage at the GM’s discretion.

substitutions.replacement: You may choose to create a bubble barrier. If you do, no physical objects or entities can pass into your bubble until the barrier is broken, but you and the barrier cannot move.

temporal-bubble.description: Time is effectively frozen in your Stash. Sapient targets still experience a local passage of time inside the stash, and this does not extend the duration of Effects inside the stash.

The time reduction on non-Movement Actions applies only to the parts of actions when your personal speed is a factor,: pouring water or smoking a steak are not affected, but constructing a shelter or picking a lock would be. substitutions.replacement: If this Artifact is used by someone other than its true owner, its true owner is alerted and learns the user's appearance, direction, and distance at that moment. The true owner may use a single investigation Effect on them once at any range.

alpha.name: Alpha
alpha.description: Any creature under your control through this Effect cannot be commanded or controlled by anyone else, even using other Effects.

substitutions.replacement: Any creature under your control through this Effect cannot be commanded or controlled by anyone else, even using other Effects.

hail-storm.name: Hail Storm
hail-storm.description: You may Exert your Mind to make an attack against all targets within a 120 degree arc in front of you. Your full attack Outcome applies to each of them, but they may attempt to Dodge or Defend if they are aware of the attack.

substitutions.replacement: Hail Storm: You may Exert your Mind to make an attack with [[weapon-type]] against all targets within a 120 degree arc in front of you. Your full attack Outcome applies to each of them, but they may attempt to Dodge or Defend if they are aware of the attack.

flying-sword.name: Flying Sword
flying-sword.description: Animated objects are able to take offensive actions in combat. When multiple animated objects attack together, they use the mob rules.
flying-sword.eratta: If the object is a weapon, it receives bonus damage based on their weapon stats. GMs may add additional damage bonuses (or reductions) at their discretion depending on the size and material composition of the animated object. A hulking, car-sized statue might get a damage bonus of +4 if it hits, whereas a feather duster might have their damage capped at 0.

substitutions.replacement: Animated objects are able to take offensive actions in combat. If they are a weapon, they receive bonus damage based on their weapon stats. GMs may add additional damage bonuses (or reductions) at their discretion depending on the size and material composition of the animated object.
A hulking, car-sized statue might get a damage bonus of +4 if it hits, whereas a feather duster might have their damage capped at 0. Multiple attacking animated objects use mob rules.

electroreception.name: Electroreception
electroreception.description: Electroreception: You may detect electromagnetic fields, electric currents, and even neurological activity in a 50 foot radius from yourself at all times. When detecting neurological activity, you learn nothing but the creature's rough size.
electroreception.eratta: When you are within 50 feet of range, your electroreception sense is sufficient for aiming.

substitutions.replacement: Electroreception: You may detect electromagnetic fields, electric currents, and even neurological activity in a 50 foot radius from yourself at all times. When detecting neurological activity, you learn nothing but the creature's rough size.
When you are within 50 feet of range, your electroreception sense is sufficient for aiming.

dream-seeker.description: You learn where the target sleeps, how long they tend to sleep each night, whether or not they have nightmares, and what their nightmares tend to be about.

substitutions.replacement: You learn where the target sleeps, how long they tend to sleep each night, whether or not they have nightmares, and what their nightmares tend to be about.

acute-self-care.name: Acute Self-Care
acute-self-care.description: You may convert one of your Traumas into a Condition in order to heal all Mind Damage and Traumas gained in the last 24 hours.
acute-self-care.eratta:
If you have no existing Traumas, you may take a new Condition in order to use this enhancement. The specifics of the new Condition are subject to GM discretion.

This enhancement only cures Traumas, not Conditions.Does not work with augmentations or Traumas without downsides.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:

Acute Self-Care: You may heal any Traumas and Mind Damage received in the last 24 hours by converting an existing Trauma into a permanent Condition. If you have no existing Traumas, you may take a new permanent Condition instead.
Acute Self-Care does not work with augmentations or Traumas without downsides.

interrupt.name: Interrupt

substitutions.replacement: You may activate this Effect as a Reaction to contest any Action a valid target is taking, reducing their Outcome by your own. Your command has no impact beyond disrupting their Action. This interrupts Concentration.

got-no-life.name: Got No Life
got-no-life.description: You are considered Non-Living, and your Injuries no longer degrade with time. You do not age naturally, and supernatural attempts to age you fail.
got-no-life.eratta: Powers which can only affect Living things have no effect on you. You must still perform some equivalent of eating, sleeping, and drinking unless other Effects allow you to ignore that requirement.

substitutions.replacement: Non-Living
Your Injuries no longer degrade with time. You do not age naturally, and supernatural attempts to age you fail.

who-dunnit.name: Who Dunnit?
who-dunnit.description: If this target is destroyed or killed, you learn the name and appearance of the being responsible.

substitutions.replacement: If this target is destroyed or killed, you learn the name and appearance of the being responsible.

bedside-manner.name: Bedside Manner
bedside-manner.description: Whenever you successfully provide major medical treatment to a patient, they recover 1 Mind. A patient cannot recover more than one Mind per day in this way.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:
Bedside Manner: Whenever you successfully provide major medical treatment to a patient, they recover 1 Mind. A patient cannot recover more than one Mind per day in this way.

chain-lightning.name: Chain Lightning
chain-lightning.description: You may opt to have this Effect spread to all targets within 5 feet of the initial target, then to all unaffected targets within 5 feet of them, and so on, jumping a maximum of 5 times. No target may be hit more than once. You cannot hit yourself.

substitutions.replacement: You may opt to have this Effect spread to all targets within 5 feet of the initial target, then to all unaffected targets within 5 feet of them, and so on, jumping a maximum of 5 times. No target may be hit more than once. You cannot hit yourself.

the-veil.name: The Veil
the-veil.description: Sapient beings outside the zone that witness an Alien phenomena inside it will not see it as Alien in nature, instead “filling in the blank” with some mundane explanation for what they’re seeing. Also affects recording devices in and out of the zone.
the-veil.eratta: Mundane phenomena can still be alarming. For example, instead of seeing someone summon chain lightning from their hands, they may see them opening up with an automatic rifle. Sapient beings inside the zone are unaffected.

substitutions.replacement: Mundane phenomena can still be alarming. For example, instead of seeing someone summon chain lightning from their hands, they may see them opening up with an automatic rifle.

hypothetically-speaking.name: Hypothetically Speaking. . .
hypothetically-speaking.description: Once per Contract you may Exert your Mind to ask the GM question of the form “would X happen if Y?” and receive an answer of “yes,” “no,” “probably,” or “probably not”.
hypothetically-speaking.eratta: For example, you could ask “would it be bad for my health if I touch the magic orb?” Or “would I be able to issue a self-destruct order to the space ship if I found the control panel?” The GM must answer with as much certainty as they can.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:

gains-before-brains.name: Gains Before Brains
gains-before-brains.description: You may spend an Action and recover any amount of Mind Damage. Take an Injury equal to the amount of Mind Damage recovered that cannot be reduced by any means or healed within one day.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:

Gains Before Brains: You may spend an Action and recover any amount of Mind Damage. Take an Injury equal to the amount of Mind Damage recovered that cannot be reduced by any means or healed within one day.

sherlock.name: Sherlock
sherlock.description: You may Exert your Mind to deduce a fragment of the chain of events that led to a particular perceivable detail of an individual or environment. This Effect reveals one piece of information that is not discoverable by a mundane investigation.
sherlock.eratta: You may not use this Effect on the same target more than once in a 24 hour period. The amount of information received is limited at the GM's discretion but should at least contain some information that would not be learned from a mundane investigation.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:
Sherlock: The amount of information received is limited at the GM's discretion but should at least contain some information that would not be learned from a mundane investigation.

Sherlock: You may Exert your Mind to deduce a fragment of the chain of events that led to a particular perceivable detail of an individual or environment. This Effect reveals one piece of information that is not discoverable by a mundane investigation.

other-obscure.name: Other
other-obscure.description: You are obscured from two senses that are uncommon to humans such as thermal vision, electroreception, radar, echolocation, aura-sight, or something else.
other-obscure.eratta: This can prevent detection via Effects when specifically flavor-appropriate. Requires Playgroup Leader approval.

substitutions.replacement: Other: $

autopilot.name: Autopilot
autopilot.description: While possessing your target, you may put them into “autopilot.” During this time they will act as they would normally for the situation without the knowledge that you are/were possessing them.
autopilot.eratta: This allows them to act “normal” around social contacts, type in passwords if they want to log in, etc. You have no control during this time.

mine-now.name: Mine Now
mine-now.description: Only you can free an Imprisoned target.

substitutions.replacement: Only you can free an Imprisoned target.

legerdemain.name: Legerdemain
legerdemain.description: You can store and withdraw items as a Quick Action. You may also choose to equip any equipment you withdraw.
legerdemain.eratta: "Equip" refers to drawing a gun, putting on a piece of armor, etc. You may store or withdraw a maximum of 6 items in a given Round. Reminder: you may perform up to two Quick Actions for -2 dice each or 3 if you use up your Action, unless a different Effect grants you additional Quick Actions.

substitutions.replacement: spend a Quick Action
You may choose to equip any equipment you withdraw at no additional cost.

sig-bound.name: Tether
sig-bound.description: Whenever this Artifact is unattended and within 300 feet of its true owner, it will fly back to them. When not held or stowed, it floats beside its true owner, moving with them. While within 10 feet, the true owner does not need to touch it to use it
sig-bound.eratta: The Artifact can fly at a maximum speed of 100 feet per Round and only towards its true owner. This does not grant the Artifact its own Action or Reaction, and it moves at the same time as its true owner’s Movement.

substitutions.replacement: Whenever this Artifact is unattended and within 300 feet of its true owner, it will fly back to them. When not held or stowed, it floats beside its true owner, moving with them. While within 10 feet, the true owner does not need to touch it to use it.

burn-the-unworthy.name: Burn the Unworthy
burn-the-unworthy.description: There is a specific type of being that cannot hold or use this Artifact. They take a Severity 1 Injury each Round they touch or possess of the Artifact. It cannot be prevented or healed by any means until one day after they relinquish possession.
burn-the-unworthy.eratta: For example, the undead, murderers, religious people, non-humans, etc. If the true owner ever falls into these categories, they cease to be the Artifact’s true owner.

substitutions.replacement: $ cannot hold or use this Artifact. They take a Severity 1 Injury each Round they touch or possess of the Artifact. It cannot be prevented or healed by any means until one day after they relinquish possession.

next-of-kin.name: Next of Kin
next-of-kin.description: If the true owner of this Artifact ever dies, it immediately disappears. At least one week later, it will appear to a qualifying next of kin and reveal its power to them. They become its new true owner.
next-of-kin.eratta: Qualification cannot name any specific individual or the Artifact’s creator. Examples include: a British child of upstanding moral quality, a descendant of the royal bloodline, or someone who once saved the last true owner’s life. If there is no available Next of Kin, it will wait two months and then appear to a random person. If multiple next of kin are appropriate, the GM will decide randomly.

stutterstep.name: Stutterstep
stutterstep.description: Instead of spending your full Action to activate this Effect, you may activate it and then split it into multiple sub-hops, with each one costing a Quick Action. You must make all hops before or after your main Action.
stutterstep.eratta: In total, you cannot move more than your maximum jump distance with these hops in a Round.

lets-do-this.name: Let's Do This
lets-do-this.description: When you roll initiative, you may Exert your Mind before you roll to ignore Stress and get +2 to your Outcome.

substitutions.replacement: When you roll initiative, you may Exert your Mind before you roll to ignore Stress and get +2 to your Outcome.

out-sight.name: Out of Sight
out-sight.description: You may use this Effect to take non-Combat Actions on targets which are out of sight, provided you have an understanding of their location.
out-sight.eratta: Using this Effect on targets which you cannot see will not give you any descriptive information about their location or surroundings, though you will have a sense of whether or not you are “touching” them, or if they bump into something.

substitutions.replacement: Using this Effect on targets which you cannot see will not give you any descriptive information about their location or surroundings, though you will have a sense of whether or not you are “touching” them, or if they bump into something.

rebar.name: Rebar
rebar.description: After activating this Effect, you may maintain Concentration for 3 Rounds to reinforce your alteration. If you do, it becomes as sturdy as concrete and just as difficult to break.

substitutions.replacement: After activating this Effect, you may maintain Concentration for 3 Rounds to reinforce your alteration. If you do, it becomes as sturdy as concrete and just as difficult to break.

launch.name: Launch
launch.description: As long as you are outside, you may Exert your Mind and spend an Action to throw any Object you can lift without Concentrating into orbit.

substitutions.replacement: As long as you are outside, you may Exert your Mind and spend an Action to throw any Object you can lift without Concentrating into orbit.

flow-water.name: Flow Like Water
flow-water.description: If you win a Clash, you may redirect the incoming Attack to another target within its original range using your Clash Outcome as its own. You may Clash against an Attack as long as the attacker, target, or line of fire is within arm’s reach.
flow-water.eratta: If you fail a Clash on behalf of another target, the target may choose to React as normal to the original Attack.

substitutions.replacement: You also gain the following effects:

Flow Like Water: If you win a Clash, you may redirect the incoming Attack to another target within its original range using your Clash Outcome as its own. You may Clash against an Attack as long as the attacker, target, or line of fire is within arm’s reach.

transcendence.name: Transcendence
transcendence.description: When Defending, you may Exert your Mind to Defend against any number of incoming Attacks for the rest of the Round.
transcendence.eratta: When Defending against a mob, you do not suffer the Difficulty Penalty, and their Attack does not gain the normal Outcome bonus.

Transcendence: When Defending, you may Exert your Mind to Defend against any number of incoming Attacks for the rest of the Round. When Defending against a mob, you do not suffer the Difficulty Penalty, and their Attack does not gain the normal Outcome bonus.

catcher-rye.name: Catcher In The Rye
catcher-rye.description: You may catch anything flying within arm’s reach without a roll and without using a Reaction, as long as it is not an Attack. You may juggle a number of throwable objects equal to your Dexterity as a Free Action.

bunny-hops.name: Bunny Hops
bunny-hops.description: This Effect has no activation cost the first 10 times you activate in each minute.

substitutions.replacement: If you have activated this Effect 10 times in the last minute, you must Exert your Mind.

full-spectrum.name: Full Spectrum
full-spectrum.description: You can perceive things from your wards via all of your senses, including enhanced senses. You may destroy the Ward to activate one Investigate Individual, Object, or Area Effect as though you were standing at its location.
full-spectrum.eratta: This investigation Effect is as obvious as it would normally be. The Effect is targeted as though you were standing at the location of the Ward. You must pay all activation costs and satisfy all activation requirements of the Effect.

flock.name: Flock
flock.description: You may affect up to 3 targets within range.

substitutions.replacement: You may affect up to 3 targets within range.

forced-conjoinment.name: Forced Conjoinment
forced-conjoinment.description: If two or more of your targets are touching and more than one fails to resist, you may conjoin them where they touch. This counts as a shared Battle Scar.
forced-conjoinment.eratta: While merged, the targets share a Movement and move at the speed of the slowest of the group. All physical Actions they take are +Contested Outcome Difficulty (lowest of the group). If one dies while merged or if forcefully separated, the others take an Injury equal to the lowest Contested Outcome + 2.

specialized.name: Specialized
specialized.description: Your Armor only defends against a single, narrowly defined, type of Damage, but it does it exceptionally well, granting you an additional +3 Armor, and cancelling any armor piercing effects from that damage.
specialized.eratta: Example classes of damage include: Bullets, Hand-to-hand attacks, edged weapons, blunt weapons, attacks from a range greater than 150 feet, heat-based damage, electrical damage, etc.

substitutions.replacement: Armor, which only reduces Damage taken from $.  Any Armor piercing Effects from $ are ignored.

pager-duty.name: Pager Duty
pager-duty.description: You automatically detect incoming Alien hack attempts on your secured systems. You may communicate with hackers as long as they are actively attempting to hack you, and you can roll Perception + Technology after the hack to determine its impact.
pager-duty.eratta: While communicating with the hacker, each of you may present yourselves in any way you wish.

substitutions.replacement: You automatically detect incoming Alien hack attempts on your secured systems. You may communicate with hackers as long as they are actively attempting to hack you, and you can roll Perception + Technology after the hack to determine its impact.

dox.name: Dox
dox.description: Whenever an Alien attempt to hack one of your secured systems is made, you learn the location of the hacker and can recognize any devices used in the hack if you see them later. When you attempt to hack a target via a network, you learn its location.

substitutions.replacement: Whenever an Alien attempt to hack one of your secured systems is made, you learn the location of the hacker and can recognize any devices used in the hack if you see them later. When you attempt to hack a target via a network, you learn its location.

bubble-shield.name: Bubble Shield
bubble-shield.description: Your armor protects everyone within a 15 foot radius from Attacks, Effects, and projectiles originating outside the radius. However, any Damage originating from inside the radius is not affected.

substitutions.replacement: Your armor protects everyone within a 15 foot radius from Attacks, Effects, and projectiles originating outside the radius. However, any Damage originating from inside the radius is not affected.  [[intersecting-shield-errata]]

People may move through your bubble shield without issue. Your bubble shield follows its original target. Those outside of the bubble shield radius do not receive any Armor from Attacks originating within the bubble shield.

battery-operated.name: Battery-operated
battery-operated.description: Instead of Exerting Mind to activate this Effect, it has its own 3-point Source pool. The Source regenerates at the rate of one point per day and cannot be raised with Experience.
battery-operated.eratta: Different Effects on the same Artifact with this Enhancement share a battery pool. Each additional Battery-Operated after the first increases the capacity of the Source by 3, to a maximum of 12.

It has a Battery pool with ##additional-battery++ points of Battery. Up to one missing point of Battery will regenerate each day.

distributed-systems.name: Distributed Systems
distributed-systems.description: No single organ in your body is critical for life. Called Shots do no additional Damage to you. You may perceive the world through separated body parts, take Actions with them, and reattach them by spending an Action and Exerting your Mind.
distributed-systems.eratta: Detached parts move at 5 feet per Round. You may activate awareness Effects through detached sensory organs. This does not grant extra Actions. Detaching a body part always causes a Battle Scar, and the detached part "dies" if the Battle Scar is healed.

yeet.name: Yeet!
yeet.description: You can throw things up to 100 extra feet per point of Brawn with no penalty. Anything thrown further than 200 feet will not land at its target until the next Round on your initiative.
yeet.eratta: When throwing further than 200 feet, you may throw where you expect a stationary or predictably-moving target to be, but if they move in an unexpected way, the Attack will miss.

champion-caber-toss.name: Champion of the Caber Toss
champion-caber-toss.description: You may lift an additional 2000 pounds times your rating in Extra Brawn, and you may throw items up to this weight to a distance of your throwing roll’s Outcome x10 feet.
champion-caber-toss.eratta: Weapon Damage for a heavy improvised thrown weapon is equal to the Brawn required to lift it minus 2 (minimum 0).

the-ol-switcheroo.name: The 'Ol Switcheroo
the-ol-switcheroo.description: If you and your target weigh within 500 pounds of each other, you may swap positions with them.
the-ol-switcheroo.eratta: Both you and the target must each be under the maximum weight you can move.

family-vacation.description: You may bring one additional Animate being with you as long as you are touching them. They may Resist unless they are grappled.

eye-of-the-past.description: When specifying a time for looking back into the past, instead of selecting a date and time, you may view the most recent time a particular event occurred in the area.
eye-of-the-past.eratta: For example, the last time someone transformed into a werewolf, the last time Lucy punched someone, etc

ammo-no-damage.description: This consumable is meant to be used as ammo in a ranged weapon, applied to a melee weapon as poison, or to be thrown. It uses the standard attack roll and range for the selected attack type. The attack itself does no damage, but the Effect may.
ammo-no-damage.eratta: Attack types include: firearms, thrown, bow-and-arrow, sling, etc. The Attack roll used to activate this Consumable cannot be at a Difficulty lower than 6. You may only use one Consumable per Action.

group-therapy-mend-trauma.name: Group Therapy
group-therapy-mend-trauma.description: You may target a number of individuals equal to your Charisma. Any Traumas you treat are revealed to the entire group.
group-therapy-mend-trauma.eratta: This Enhancement can be used with the Enhancement Suppress, but not with the Enhancement Stabilize.

environmental-adaptation.name: Natural Environment
environmental-adaptation.description: There is a particular hostile environment that you are uniquely suited to survive in. Heat, cold, pressure, radiation, and other native hazards do not affect you. Dangerous local flora and fauna will not bother you unless you give them a reason.

substitutions.replacement: While in $, heat, cold, pressure, radiation, and other native hazards do not affect you. Dangerous local flora and fauna will not bother you unless you give them a reason.

non-combatant.description: The Armor’s rating is increased by 2. If you do something that Damages, restricts, or otherwise directly, negatively affects another being, your Armor’s rating is decreased by 3 for one minute. This penalty does not stack with itself.

interface.name: Interface
interface.description: If your command does not require bypassing the security of the target (for example, typing on a computer that is unlocked), you do not need to Exert your Mind to activate this Effect, and activation only takes a single Action.

different-body.name: Different Body
different-body.description: The Injuries and Battle Scars of your Alternate Form and your true form are tracked separately.
different-body.eratta: If a given individual has access to this Enhancement via multiple Gifts or Artifacts, all different bodies share the same Injuries and Battle Scars.

slapdash.description: You may opt to have your alteration form immediately. If you do, it reverts at the end of your initiative next Round.

leave-no-trace.name: Leave No Trace
leave-no-trace.description: Your projectiles crumble or vanish after impact, leaving no meaningful trace of your assault beyond the wounds they leave. If your attack would kill or incapacitate your target, they make no sound, even incidentally, for the next Round.

spirit-paths.name: Spirit Paths
spirit-paths.description: If you traverse a specific type of environment for one minute, you may activate this Effect and travel to the edge of any other environment of that type that you’re aware of, regardless of range.
spirit-paths.eratta: Eg you can walk through a graveyard and end up in a graveyard halfway across the world.

wall-of-fire.name: Wall of Fire
wall-of-fire.description: When an Alien attempt to hack your secure system fails, you may select one of several retaliatory actions to make against the attacker.
